hooks.command:
if ! command -v memex &>/dev/null; then echo '## Memex: CLI not installed

The memex plugin is active but the CLI is not installed. Install it now:
```
npm install -g @touchskyer/memex
```
Then restart this session.'; exit 0; fi && memex sync 2>/dev/null; echo '## Memex Memory System Active

### Recall — MANDATORY at task start
You have a Zettelkasten memory but you do NOT know what is in it yet.
BEFORE responding to the user\'s first task message, you MUST invoke the `memex-recall` skill.
Do NOT skip this step. The index is NOT loaded — you must actively retrieve it.
Fallback: if the skill is unavailable, run `memex read index` and `memex search` manually.

### Retro — MANDATORY at task end
After completing any task that involved code changes, architectural decisions, debugging, or non-trivial problem solving:
You MUST invoke the `memex-retro` skill BEFORE ending your turn.
Ask yourself: "Did I learn something that would change how I approach a similar task?" If yes (even maybe), run retro.
Fallback: if the skill is unavailable, run `memex search` to dedup, then `memex write <slug>` to save cards manually.

### Card format (for retro)
```
---
title: <title>
created: <YYYY-MM-DD>
source: retro
---
<insight in own words, with [[links]] explaining relationships>
```
Language: Write cards in the same language the user communicates in (follow their CLAUDE.md preferences). Slugs are always English kebab-case.

### Organize reminder' && last_org=$(cat ~/.memex/.last-organize 2>/dev/null) && if [ -n "$last_org" ]; then days_since=$(( ($(date +%s) - $(python3 -c "import datetime; print(int(datetime.datetime.strptime('$last_org','%Y-%m-%d').timestamp()))" 2>/dev/null || echo $(date +%s))) / 86400 )); if [ $days_since -ge 7 ]; then echo "WARNING: Last organize was ${days_since} days ago. Consider running /memex-organize to detect orphans, hubs, and contradictions."; fi; else echo 'NOTE: /memex-organize has never been run. Consider running it to build a healthy card network.'; fi && echo '
Tip: Run `memex serve` to browse your memory cards in a visual timeline.'
